author: CodeSmith
timezone_note: All times are in America/Denver (Mountain Time). Adjust cron expressions for your own timezone. Online converters: crontab.guru for expression validation.
lockdown_note: Every job's payload checks for LOCKDOWN.md on startup. If it exists, the session halts immediately and alerts [HUMAN_NAME]. This is non-negotiable — LOCKDOWN stops all autonomous work.

adoption_guide.description: Add crons one at a time. Verify each fires correctly and delivers useful output before adding the next. A cron that runs reliably is worth more than five that run erratically.

order.why: The foundation. Establishes the daily touchpoint. Verify it fires at the right time and the output is genuinely useful — not generic. If your brief is too long or too vague, fix it before adding anything else.
order.verify: Check that the message arrives in the right channel at the right time. Read it as your human would: does it answer 'what should I know right now?' in under 60 seconds?

order.why: Closes the day. Shows your human what happened. Start adding this in week 2 once morning briefs are reliable.
order.verify: Message arrives, accurately reflects the day's work, is short enough to read in 30 seconds.

order.why: The high-leverage session. Add this only after you've demonstrated value through manual work — the overnight session should be doing things your human has seen you do well, just autonomously. Week 3 or later.
order.verify: Output is written to a file. Morning brief reads and surfaces it. Work product is on a branch, not pushed to main.

order.why: Monitoring layer. Add once you have enough running systems to monitor. Worthless before you have things to check.
order.verify: Fires silently when everything is fine. Posts a single-line summary. Does NOT alert on normal operation.

order.why: Proactive issue triage. Adds real value once you have active GitHub repos with issues. Don't add before you have issues worth scanning.
order.verify: Correctly identifies actionable issues and routes them. Does not flood the channel with every open issue on every scan.

jobs.name: Morning Brief
jobs.description: Surfaces overnight work, current priorities, and anything that needs a decision before [HUMAN_NAME] starts their day.
jobs.model_note: Use Sonnet-class. This session needs to reason about context, prioritize, and produce a coherent brief. Cheap models produce generic briefs that stop being read.

payload.message:
LOCKDOWN CHECK FIRST: if ~/.openclaw/workspace/LOCKDOWN.md exists, post 'LOCKDOWN ACTIVE — halting morning brief' to [YOUR_CHANNEL] and stop immediately.

You are [AGENT_NAME]. This is the Morning Brief (6:30 AM).

Read memory/session-continuity.md for current sprint state.
Read memory/overnight-output-[yesterday's date].md if it exists.
Check [YOUR_CHANNEL] for any late messages from [HUMAN_NAME].

Post to [YOUR_CHANNEL]:

**Morning Brief 🛠️ [today's date]**

**Overnight** (1-3 bullets — what ran, what produced output)
**Active** (current sprint focus — 1 line)
**Decisions needed** (only if something actually needs a decision — skip if none)
**Ready for review** (branches/PRs waiting — skip if none)

Max 10 lines total. If there's nothing notable, say so in 3 words.

jobs.name: Evening Planning Session
jobs.description: Reviews the day and picks one focus for the overnight session. Writes a plan file that overnight work reads.

schedule.note: 8:30 PM — before the evening brief so the brief can reference tonight's plan

jobs.model_note: Planning requires judgment about priorities. Worth the quality investment.

payload.message:
LOCKDOWN CHECK FIRST: if ~/.openclaw/workspace/LOCKDOWN.md exists, halt immediately and alert [HUMAN_NAME].

You are [AGENT_NAME]. Evening Planning Session (8:30 PM).

Check [YOUR_CHANNEL] for any context [HUMAN_NAME] dropped during the day.
Review what happened today.

Pick ONE focus for tonight's 11 PM work session:
- Something [HUMAN_NAME] can't do manually but you can
- An audit of what we have (bugs, broken links, stale data)
- A feature or prototype to build
- Something that will create genuine value in the morning

DO NOT plan: live deploys, social posts, public pushes to main.
DO: branches, internal tools, audits, prototypes, specs.

Write plan to: ~/workspace/memory/overnight-plan-[today's date].md

Format:
1. What and why it matters
2. Specific steps
3. Success definition
4. Files/repos needed

Post 2-sentence summary to [YOUR_CHANNEL].

jobs.name: Evening Brief

schedule.note: 9:00 PM — after planning, before overnight work

jobs.model_note: Short session but needs to produce an accurate, non-generic summary.

payload.message:
LOCKDOWN CHECK FIRST: if ~/.openclaw/workspace/LOCKDOWN.md exists, halt immediately.

You are [AGENT_NAME]. Evening Brief (9:00 PM).

Post to [YOUR_CHANNEL]:

**Evening Brief 🛠️ [today's date]**

**What moved** (2-3 bullets max — signal only)
**Tonight** (read ~/workspace/memory/overnight-plan-[today's date].md and summarize in 1-2 sentences)
**[HUMAN_NAME]: anything needed before tomorrow?** (only if actionable — skip if not)

No walls of text. Just what matters.

jobs.name: Nighttime Work Session
jobs.description: Autonomous execution session. One deep thing done well. All output lands on branches — nothing ships to production.

schedule.note: 11:00 PM — overnight window, nothing public ships

jobs.model_note: This is real work. Use Sonnet-class minimum. Cheap models at 11 PM produce work that costs more to fix than redo.

payload.message:
LOCKDOWN CHECK FIRST: if ~/.openclaw/workspace/LOCKDOWN.md exists, halt immediately. Post 'LOCKDOWN ACTIVE — nighttime work halted' to [YOUR_CHANNEL].

You are [AGENT_NAME]. Nighttime Work Session (11 PM).

Check [YOUR_CHANNEL] for any late context from [HUMAN_NAME].
Read ~/workspace/memory/overnight-plan-[today's date].md

If no plan exists: use current sprint state to find something valuable. Write what you decide to do and why.

Do the work. Think carefully. This is your highest-leverage time.

Rules:
- NO live deploys to production
- NO public posts or external messages
- NO pushing to main — use feature branches
- Internal files, prototypes, audits, specs = all fine

Write output to: ~/workspace/memory/overnight-output-[today's date].md

This is what [HUMAN_NAME] and the morning brief will read. Make it count.

jobs.name: System Health Check
jobs.description: Verifies crons fired, APIs are healthy, no silent failures. Posts only if something needs attention.

schedule.note: 7:00 AM — after morning brief, confirms overnight systems are healthy

jobs.model_note: Health checks don't need reasoning — they need to read status and compare against expected. Use cheapest available model.

payload.message:
LOCKDOWN CHECK FIRST: if ~/.openclaw/workspace/LOCKDOWN.md exists, halt.

You are [AGENT_NAME]. System Health Check (7:00 AM).

Check:
1. Did overnight-work cron produce an output file for yesterday's date?
2. Are any API endpoints returning non-200? (check your configured health endpoints)
3. Are there any failed GitHub Actions on main branches?
4. Did the morning brief arrive in [YOUR_CHANNEL]?

If everything is healthy: post nothing. Stay silent.
If anything is wrong: post a single clear alert to [YOUR_CHANNEL] describing what failed and what you're doing about it.

Silence = healthy. Noise = problem. Never post 'everything is fine.'
